name: Qwen Code
description: Alibaba Qwen team's open-source terminal agent optimized for Qwen coder models; Apache-2.0.

developer.comment: Built and maintained by the Qwen team at Alibaba Cloud.

first-release.comment: Open-sourced in July 2025 alongside the Qwen3-Coder model; a fork of Google's Gemini CLI adapted for Qwen coder models.

status.comment: Actively developed; v0.17.x released early June 2026 (checked 2026-06-05).

license.comment: Apache-2.0 license on the agent's own source.

specialization.comment: Broad terminal coding agent; optimized for Qwen3-Coder models but general-purpose in use.

interface.comment: An open-source AI coding agent that lives in your terminal; installed via npm. Docs note IDE companion integrations (VS Code, JetBrains, Zed) but the agent itself is a CLI.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal. IDE companion integrations exist but the primary host is the shell.

default-model-provider.comment: Default/flagship is Alibaba's own Qwen3-Coder, but the tool is multi-protocol and works with OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini-compatible APIs, so provider is effectively multiple. Qwen is not a defined tag option.

model-choice.comment: Switch models at runtime via /model; supports OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini-compatible providers configured in settings.json.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Supply your own API key (Alibaba Cloud Model Studio, OpenRouter, Fireworks, or any compatible provider).

local-model-support.comment: Ollama and vLLM local model setups documented (e.g. Qwen3:32b).

multi-file-editing.comment: Agentic terminal workflow edits across files.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Chains edit/run/fix steps toward a goal; supports YOLO mode (--yolo) for minimal-intervention runs.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands as part of its loop, optionally inside a Docker sandbox.

mcp-support.comment: Supports MCP servers via mcpServers configuration (inherited from Gemini CLI lineage).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Built-in SubAgents tool for a full agentic workflow; release notes mention a fork subagent feature.

codebase-indexing.comment: Reads files on demand into context (Gemini CLI lineage); no built semantic embeddings index documented.

planning-mode.comment: Release notes describe iterative planning prompt discipline and plan-oriented workflows.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image input; vision switching happens automatically when images are detected.

browser-computer-use.comment: Computer use is zero-config built-in via the open-computer-use MCP server (added in v0.17.x, checked 2026-06-05).

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Model-driven by default: the main agent proactively delegates to subagents on its own initiative based on the request and each subagent's description/tools, without the user naming one. Explicit by-name invocation is also supported but optional (checked 2026-06-14).

operating-mode.comment: Interactive terminal chat with per-action approval (supervised) and a YOLO autonomous mode. No tab inline completion; not an async/background cloud agent.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal agent; no as-you-type tab completion.

background-async-execution.comment: Runs in a foreground terminal session; not a fire-and-forget cloud/background agent.

persistent-memory.comment: QWEN.md context files persist project/user memory across sessions; /memory refresh reloads them.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Session checkpointing for recovery via the enable-checkpointing setting (Gemini CLI lineage).

git-integration.comment: Git-aware agent; can run git commands via shell execution.

pr-creation.comment: No documented native PR-creation feature; could shell out to gh/git but no first-class integration found (checked 2026-06-05).

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Project-level QWEN.md instruction files (configurable context.fileName), with @file.md import support.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers, custom slash commands, and a Skills framework.

permission-model.comment: Default per-action approval; optional --yolo auto-run; optional Docker sandbox for shell/file ops; posture is configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Open-source CLI; runs entirely on the user's machine and can target local models (Ollama/vLLM) with no vendor cloud.

privacy-mode.comment: When pointed at a local model or self-managed API key, code stays under the user's control; no mandatory vendor telemetry of code for training documented.

enterprise-controls.comment: The open-source CLI itself ships no SSO/RBAC/audit/VPC governance; such controls would come from the chosen API provider (e.g. Alibaba Cloud), not the tool.

pricing-model.comment: The agent is free and open source; you supply a key (BYOK / usage-based via API) or subscribe to Alibaba Cloud's Coding Plan for hosted Qwen models.

free-tier.comment: The tool is free to run with your own key or a local model. Note: the vendor-hosted Qwen OAuth free tier was discontinued 2026-04-15 (checked 2026-06-05).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Tool is free/open-source and runs at zero plan cost with BYOK or local models. Hosted Qwen models require usage-based API billing or the Coding Plan (Pro $50/mo for new subscribers as of 2026-06-05; the Lite plan stopped accepting new subscriptions 2026-03-20).

maturity.comment: Usable and active since mid-2025 but rapidly evolving (still 0.x; frequent releases); solid but changing.

community-size.comment: Significant traction as the official Qwen CLI but below Cursor/Copilot/Claude Code scale (checked 2026-06-05).

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: MCP server ecosystem plus a Skills framework provide meaningful third-party extensibility.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads QWEN.md by default (a vendor-specific filename → custom); the context filename is configurable, so AGENTS.md can be used too.

scoped-rules-files.comment: QWEN.md context files are always applied (with modular @file.md imports); no documented path/glob or file-type conditional scoping mechanism.

auto-memory.comment: Memory is via manually authored QWEN.md context files; no documented autonomous cross-session memory-writing by the agent.

skill-support.comment: Ships a Skills framework (rich built-in tools: Skills, SubAgents) plus custom slash commands.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills/commands invocable by the user (slash commands) and loaded by the model as part of the agentic workflow.

always-on-surface.comment: Skills framework confirmed but the progressive-disclosure context level (name/description/full) is not documented clearly enough to assign (checked 2026-06-05).

skill-distribution.comment: Bundled skills ship with the tool and skills/commands are configured via local files; no curated marketplace/registry documented.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Opt-in Docker/Podman container sandbox (--sandbox / QWEN_SANDBOX / tools.sandbox, configurable image and custom .qwen/sandbox.Dockerfile) plus macOS Seatbelt OS sandbox (SEATBELT_PROFILE: permissive-open restricts writes to the project folder, strict denies by default). Sandbox is off by default; not Linux-kernel (Landlock/seccomp) enforced. Gemini CLI lineage.

permission-granularity.comment: permissions.allow/ask/deny rules support tool names, command-prefix patterns (Bash(git *) with word-boundary matching, Bash(npm run *)), per-path globs (Read(/src/**/*.ts)), per-domain web fetch (WebFetch(api.example.com)), and Read vs Edit meta-categories (read_file/grep/glob/list vs edit/write_file) for separate read/write gating.

rule-precedence.comment: Decision priority deny > ask > allow > default/interactive; deny always wins. No documented specificity-based nested exceptions or user-ordered evaluation.

permission-configurability.comment: User-authored allow/ask/deny rules in settings.json plus approval-mode presets. No documented programmable per-action permission hook/policy callback for the permission decision itself (Gemini CLI lineage).

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No out-of-the-box deny of .env/secret/key files. advanced.excludedEnvVars only excludes env vars (DEBUG/DEBUG_MODE by default) from context, which is not access gating; users must author their own permissions.deny rule to block secret-file access (checked 2026-06-12).

persistent-allowlist.comment: permissions.allow/ask/deny rules are saved in settings.json and persist across sessions, so approved tools/commands are not re-prompted.

unsupervised-mode.comment: Documented yolo approval mode (--approval-mode yolo / --yolo / tools.approvalMode) auto-approves all tool calls for hands-off runs.

network-access-control.comment: Outbound is unrestricted by default but configurable: a top-level proxy setting (or --proxy) routes traffic through a gateway, and WebFetch(domain) permission rules can allowlist/deny specific hosts. No global egress block-by-default for shell commands.

default-permission-posture.comment: Default approval mode requires approval before modifications/commands (interactive prompt for unspecified tools); auto-edit and yolo are opt-in. First-run posture is prompt-each-action.

auto-accept-mode.comment: In-session auto-edit mode (⏵⏵ accept edits on) auto-approves file edits (edit/write_file/notebook_edit) while still prompting for shell commands. Toggled quickly via Shift+Tab (Tab on Windows) or /approval-mode auto-edit — the middle rung between per-action prompting and the full yolo bypass (Gemini CLI lineage; checked 2026-07-18).
